user.description: Enter your ENGIE Belgium credentials and two-factor authentication method. **A dedicated ENGIE account is required**: signing into the same account from engie.be or the ENGIE Smart App appears to revoke the integration's session and force you to re-authenticate. Create a separate user via the [ENGIE user management page]({user_management_url}). New accounts have 2FA enabled by default.

data_description.username: Your ENGIE Belgium account email address.
data_description.mfa_method: Choose how you want to receive your verification code.

mfa_sms.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was sent to your phone via SMS.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

mfa_email.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was sent to your email address.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

reauth_confirm.title: Reauthenticate ENGIE Belgium
reauth_confirm.description: Your stored ENGIE access has expired for {username}. Choose how you want to receive a new verification code to re-authenticate. If this prompt keeps reappearing, the most common cause is sharing your ENGIE account between this integration and engie.be or the ENGIE Smart App. A dedicated ENGIE account is required for this integration. See the integration documentation.

data_description.mfa_method: Choose how you want to receive your verification code.

reauth_mfa.title: Enter verification code
reauth_mfa.description: Enter the 6-digit verification code that was just sent to you to finish re-authenticating.

data_description.code: The 6-digit code that ENGIE just sent you.

select_accounts.title: Choose business agreements
select_accounts.description: Authentication for {username} succeeded. Pick the ENGIE business agreements you want to add to Home Assistant. Each selection becomes its own device with its own sensors. You can add or remove business agreements later from the integration page.

data_description.selected_accounts: Select one or more business agreements to configure.

import_history_choice.title: Historical import
import_history_choice.description: Pick the households you want to import historical data for. Leave everything off to skip. The next screen lets you choose energy types, cost tracking, and an optional date window. Imports run in the background after setup. You still need to add the resulting statistics to the Energy dashboard yourself (see the [README](https://github.com/DaanVervacke/hass-engie-be/blob/feat/import-historical-energy-data/README.md#add-to-the-energy-dashboard) for the exact steps).

import_options.title: Historical import options
import_options.description: Optionally import historical hourly usage data from ENGIE. Each section below is one of the business agreements you ticked on the previous screen. When setup completes the import runs in the background. Later restarts and reloads are skipped automatically once data is present. You still need to add the resulting statistics to the Energy dashboard yourself (see the [README](https://github.com/DaanVervacke/hass-engie-be/blob/feat/import-historical-energy-data/README.md#add-to-the-energy-dashboard) for the exact steps). Use the 'Import historical usage' service action from Developer tools > Actions to run or re-run at any time.

data_description.import_start_date: Start of the window to import. Leave empty to walk back to the earliest known contract on this business agreement.
data_description.import_end_date: End of the window to import (inclusive). Leave empty to auto-select through today.

reconfigure.title: Reconfigure ENGIE Belgium
reconfigure.description: Update the preferred two-factor authentication method for {username}. Your stored credentials and tokens are not changed. Use **Re-authenticate** instead if your session has expired.

data_description.mfa_method: The verification method to use the next time you are prompted for re-authentication.

error.invalid_mfa_code: Invalid verification code. Please try again.
error.post_mfa_auth_failed: Sign-in could not be completed after the verification code was accepted. This is usually a temporary issue on the ENGIE side. Please cancel and start the setup again.
error.no_accounts_selected: Select at least one business agreement.

abort.reconfigure_successful: Preferred two-factor authentication method updated. Your next re-authentication will use this method.

cannot_connect.message: Cannot connect to the ENGIE Belgium API.

pre_v5_entry.title: ENGIE Belgium: re-add required
pre_v5_entry.description: Your ENGIE Belgium integration was set up on an older schema (v{version}) that can no longer be migrated automatically. Remove this entry from Settings → Devices & services and add the integration again to continue.

setup_import_failed.title: ENGIE Belgium: historical import failed
setup_import_failed.description: The setup-time historical usage import for '{title}' failed. Check the Home Assistant log for details. Use the 'Import historical usage' service action to retry.

import_history.name: Import historical usage
import_history.description: Fetch historical hourly usage data from ENGIE and import it into Home Assistant's long-term statistics for the selected business-agreements. All three energy types are selected by default, uncheck the ones you don't want. Set 'Include costs' to also import the per-hour cost (EUR) for each selected energy type. Leave both dates empty to fetch all historical data: the first run goes back to the business agreement's start date, subsequent runs only fetch the delta since the last recorded hour. Provide dates to import a specific window, existing hours inside that window are overwritten in place.

energy_type.name: Energy type
energy_type.description: Which energy types to import. Uncheck the ones you don't want.

start_date.name: Start date
start_date.description: Start of the window to import. Leave empty to auto-select.

end_date.name: End date
end_date.description: End of the window to import (inclusive). Leave empty to auto-select through today.

clear_import_history.name: Clear historical usage statistics
clear_import_history.description: Delete imported historical usage statistics for the selected business-agreement devices. All three energy types are selected by default, uncheck the ones you want to keep. Set 'Include costs' to also clear the matching cost streams. The next 'Import historical usage' call for the same device and energy type will backfill all history again from the business agreement's start date. Use this after ENGIE republishes historical data or if imported values are stale.

energy_type.name: Energy type
energy_type.description: Which energy types to clear. Uncheck the ones you want to keep.

include_costs.name: Include costs
include_costs.description: Also clear the per-hour cost (EUR) streams.

init.description: Configure the update interval for fetching energy prices from ENGIE Belgium.

data_description.update_interval: How often to fetch new price data from the ENGIE Belgium API, in minutes.

energy_offtake_superoffpeak_excl_vat.name: Energy super off-peak offtake price (excl. VAT)
